title: Run a staged deprecation and removal flow
description: Move a public SDK API through documented deprecation, runtime warnings, and a removal window before deleting it in a major release.
body: Steps: 1. Mark the API deprecated in code and docs with the replacement and a sunset version. 2. Emit a runtime/compile-time deprecation warning that points to the migration path. 3. Ship the replacement and keep the old API working through at least one minor cycle. 4. Remove only in a major version, with a migration note in the changelog. 5. Verify downstream samples compile against the replacement. Rollback point: before deleting the symbol. Validation: the deprecation warning fires, the replacement covers the old use cases, and removal lands only at a major bump documented in the changelog; deleting a public symbol in a minor is a contract break.
